m_IT1FQvE_4.title: Defense Tech Needs Longer Drone Flight Times. NEO Is Building It
m_IT1FQvE_4.description:
Defense Tech Needs Longer Drone Flight Times. NEO Is Building It | Market Frontlines with Danny Huh
Register for free for the International Investment Forum: https://ii-forum.com/

The ‘International Investment Forum’ – (‘IIF’) provides information on investment trends and ideas, covering all aspects of various industries, from top executives and managers around the world.
Join host Lyndsay Malchuk on Market Frontlines for an exclusive interview with Danny Huh, Senior Vice President of Strategy & Operations at NEO Battery Materials Ltd. (TSXV: NBM / OTCQB: NBMFF). As defense tech and physical AI demand longer flight times and faster charging, NEO is stepping up with a breakthrough silicon anode battery solution. Discover how NEO solved the historic volume expansion problem of silicon by using low-cost metallurgical grade silicon and retrofitted wet processing – cutting costs by 60-80% compared to competitors. Learn why the company pivoted from EV to drones and robotics, achieving double the flight time of Chinese batteries in live drone tests. With the US phasing out Chinese batteries by 2028-2031, NEO offers a secure, Korean-made supply chain and is already delivering prototypes to Asian military drone manufacturers. From their new 3.2-acre commercial facility to strategic defense advisors, find out how NEO plans to produce 2-4 million battery cells per year and become a recognized defense battery brand outside of China.

NEO Battery Materials, silicon anode, drone batteries, defense tech, lithium-ion battery, Danny Huh, physical AI, robotics battery, Korean battery supply chain, US defense battery, TSXV NBM, OTCQB NBMFF, battery cycle life, metallurgical grade silicon, commercial battery manufacturing, South Korea battery, drone flight time, battery energy density, supply chain security, National Defense Authorization Act

If you found this interview insightful, please like, subscribe, and hit the bell for more market analysis. Share your thoughts in the comments below! For more updates, follow us on social media and check out related videos linked below.

https://www.neobatterymaterials.com
https://www.linkedin.com/company/neo-battery-materials/
https://apaton.com
https://www.linkedin.com/company/apaton-finance-gmbh
https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/internationalinvestmentforum
https://www.youtube.com/@internationalinvestmentforum

VIDEO CHAPTERS:
00:00:13 - Introduction: The Race for Battery Dominance
00:01:06 - Welcome Danny Huh, SVP of NEO Battery Materials
00:01:47 - The Silicon Expansion Problem That Everyone Faces
00:03:20 - NEO’s Low-Cost Approach: Metallurgical Silicon & Retrofitted Processing
00:03:35 - Pivoting from EV to Drones, Robotics & Physical AI
00:05:08 - Doubling Drone Flight Time vs. Chinese Batteries
00:06:39 - Supply Chain Security: 100% Korean Sourcing
00:08:15 - Commercialization Timeline: Prototypes in 3-6 Months
00:09:32 - Future Catalysts: 2-4 Million Cells per Year from New Facility
00:11:11 - Challenges: Visibility & Building Customer Pipelines
00:12:22 - Strategy: South Korea First, Then US Defense Market
00:13:44 - Outro: Learn More at NEO Battery Materials

Host: Lyndsay Malchuk (Apaton Media). Guest: Danny Huh, Senior Vice President of Strategy & Operations at NEO Battery Materials Ltd.
Music and graphics licensed via Apaton Finance GmbH.

#SiliconAnode, #DroneBatteries, #DefenseTech, #PhysicalAI, #NEOBatteryMaterials, #TSXVNBM, #OTCQBNBMFF, #BatterySupplyChain, #KoreanBatteries, #DroneFlightTime, #LithiumIon, #BatteryEnergyDensity, #USDefense, #NDAA, #ChinaBatteryBan, #RoboticsBattery, #MetallurgicalSilicon, #BatteryManufacturing, #CommercialBattery, #MarketFrontlines

Investments in securities involve risks. Conflicts of interest exist. Further details are provided at the end of the video.

S3_5XdbSPc4.title: NEO Battery Materials on Battery Cell Production, NATO Supply Chain Security & 2026 Growth Strategy
S3_5XdbSPc4.description:
In this interview, Danny Huh, Senior VP of Operations at NEO Battery Materials Ltd. (TSX-V: NBM) (OTC: NBMFF), shares insights into the company's battery technology, supply chain security, and strategic focus on drone and defense applications, as well as how NEO Battery is positioning itself in the global battery market with manufacturing and customization strategies.

Chapters:

00:00 Introduction to NEO Battery Materials
01:57 Company Overview and History
03:47 Unique Selling Propositions
07:49 Team Background and Expertise
09:58 Future Value Catalysts

NEO Battery Materials Ltd. will be presenting at the upcoming Planet MicroCap Las Vegas 2026 powered by MicroCapClub. Register to attend here: https://www.meetmax.com/sched/event_130817/conference_home.html

For more information about NEO Battery Materials Ltd., please visit: https://neobatterymaterials.com/

The interview may contain forward looking statements about NEO Battery Materials Ltd. See NEO Battery Materials Ltd.'s periodic filings with SEDAR and the Securities and Exchange Commission for more complete information.

About NEO Battery Materials Ltd.

NEO Battery Materials is a Canadian-South Korean battery technology company focused on developing and producing silicon-enhanced lithium-ion batteries in drones, robotics, physical AI, electric vehicles, and energy storage systems. With a patent-protected, low-cost silicon manufacturing process, NEO Battery enables longer-running and ultra-fast charging properties and provides end-to-end battery solutions from materials selection, cell architecture, and process optimization. The Company aims to be a globally-leading producer of high-performance lithium-ion batteries and materials, building a secure, robust battery supply chain for Western manufacturers. For more information, please visit the Company’s website at: https://www.neobatterymaterials.com/.

Planet Microcap hosts the highest quality in-person microcap events in North America. The mission is to bring the best microcap investors, companies, and allocators together to gather, connect, and grow.; visit https://planetmicrocap.com/ to learn more about our Las Vegas and Toronto events.

This presentation is for informational purposes only and should not be construed as a recommendation to purchase or sell any security referenced herein. Planet MicroCap Holdings LLC and MicroCapClub LLC (collectively, “we” or “our”) are not licensed brokers nor registered investment advisors. We, our partners, contractors, members, subscribers, guests, or affiliates may or may not hold positions in one or more of the securities mentioned in this presentation and may trade in such securities at any time. We may have received cash compensation from one or more participants for presenting at past, present, or future events. We recommend you consult a licensed investment adviser, broker, or legal counsel before purchasing or selling any securities referenced in this presentation.

NDZAISsD430.title: Breakthrough Battery Tech: Inside Neo's Factory
NDZAISsD430.description:
Batteries could soon last ten times longer, transforming industries from defense to consumer tech. Join host Thomas Braun in South Korea to see how raw materials are turned in to cutting edge batteries. This is the first inside look at a factory at the forefront of battery innovation, revealing how a small startup is challenging industry giants and reshaping global supply chains. Why does this matter? Because batteries are the bottleneck for drone range, electric vehicle autonomy, and portable device convenience. This new breakthrough not only offers a path to longer flying hours and faster recharges but also reduces reliance on Chinese supply chains—an imperative for national defense and economic security.  Perfect for innovators, engineers, and strategic thinkers, this episode distills the science and potential of a game-changing technology.  

#BatteryTech #Innovation #SiliconAnode #NeoBattery #EnergyRevolution

J7tT1yxx5nI.title: NEO Battery Materials (TSXV:NBM) interview, June 2026
J7tT1yxx5nI.description:
Neo Battery Materials is a company listed on the TSX Venture Exchange that develops silicon-enhanced lithium-ion battery technology. The company aims to connect Canada’s resource-heavy industry with South Korea’s tech-heavy sector to create high-performance battery materials that offer faster charging, longer runtimes, and lower costs per energy unit.

Danny Huh, SVP Strategy and Operations, sat down with Cantech Letter's Amber Mac to talk about the company's plans.

6PxhtwYsQq8.title: NEO Battery Materials: Drone Batteries, Defense Technology & Non-Chinese Battery Supply Chain
6PxhtwYsQq8.description:
⚡ NEO Battery Materials is transforming the drone battery market with high-performance lithium-ion batteries designed for defense, robotics, and autonomous systems.

In this interview, Danny Huh discusses NEO Battery Material's shift to commercial battery manufacturing, growing demand for secure non-Chinese battery supply chains, performance improvements for drone batteries, and key growth catalysts including production expansion and increasing defense opportunities.

#NEOBatteryMaterials #DroneBatteries #DefenseTechnology #BatteryTechnology #LithiumIon #Drones #Robotics #BatteryManufacturing #Defense #SupplyChain
